name: Character.AI
tagline: AI chatbot platform for creating and talking to AI characters
description: Character.AI was gutted when Google paid $2.7B to rehire co-founders Noam Shazeer and Daniel De Freitas plus 30 key researchers, leaving the company as a shell.
story: Character.AI was founded in 2021 by Noam Shazeer and Daniel De Freitas, both former Google researchers. Shazeer notably co-authored the landmark 'Attention Is All You Need' paper that introduced the transformer architecture. The company built a platform where users could create and chat with AI characters, attracting millions of users, particularly young people. But Google wanted its talent back. In August 2024, Google paid approximately $2.7 billion in a licensing deal that brought co-founders Shazeer and De Freitas back to Google, along with about 30 key researchers and engineers. Character.AI continued operating under interim CEO Dominic Perella, but the company had lost its visionary leadership and core technical talent. The deal also attracted regulatory scrutiny, and the company faced mounting safety concerns around teen users, including a high-profile lawsuit following a teenager's suicide.

sources.title: Google Character AI Noam Shazeer returns

rejection_reason: Character.AI is still operating as of 2026 with 20M+ MAU and ~$50M annual revenue. The Google deal was a talent/licensing deal, not an acquisition. The product was not killed — only founders left. Does not meet failure criteria.
review_flags: Funding of $150M appears to be just the Series A round. Wikipedia indicates a $43M seed round was also raised in 2021, bringing total to ~$193M. Consider updating funding_raised to $193M.
Google deal amount of $2.7B in the description/story could not be fully verified via Wikipedia. Wikipedia confirms the deal happened in August 2024 but does not state the $2.7B figure.

IMPORTANT: Character.AI is still operating as of April 2026 with 20M+ monthly active users and ~$50M annual revenue. The product was NOT killed — only the founders/key staff left. Entry says shutdown 2024-08 but the company is alive and growing. Consider rejecting or reclassifying — the project criteria require 'product killed' for acqui-hires.
